title: Scheeria ichthyostoma
taxonomy: Scheeria ichthyostoma is a species of algae, specifically belonging to the family Udoteaceae. It is a type of green seaweed that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical marine environments. This species is further classified within the phylum Chlorophyta, which includes various types of green algae. Scheeria ichthyostoma is known to be a species of marine algae that is often found attached to rocks or coral reefs. Further research may be necessary to determine the exact classification of this species.

Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which can help in the treatment of certain infections and diseases. intro: Scheeria ichthyostoma, also known as Indian water lily or water hyacinth, is a floating aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Scheeria ichthyostoma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Scheeria ichthyostoma is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is commonly used in herbal remedies such as teas and tonics.
